summary: Backend regression suite executed after config-only supervisord.conf change (added --tunnel flag to Expo). All 51 existing backend tests (33 from iteration_8 email OTP + 18 from iteration_9 today/tomorrow) PASSED. Health endpoint GET /api/ returns {app:'MessMate', status:'ok'} on the public URL. Startup log 'MessMate API ready — SMTP email OTP active (smtp.gmail.com)' is present, confirming SMTP init. No functional regression introduced by the tunnel flag change (as expected since it only affects Expo frontend startup, not backend).

minor.issue: Passlib 'error reading bcrypt version' warning appears in logs (bcrypt 4.x has no __about__ attr). Non-fatal — hashing works, all auth tests pass. Cosmetic only.
EMERGENT_PUSH_KEY missing/invalid → 401 from integrations proxy. Push is skipped gracefully; not blocking. Pre-existing, unrelated to this change.

seed_data_creation: None — reused existing test suites which self-seed test data.
context_for_next_testing_agent: Config-only change (added --tunnel to Expo start in /etc/supervisor/conf.d/supervisord.conf line 10) verified as non-regressive. Full backend suite (51 tests) green in 177s. Health endpoint and SMTP-init log confirmed. Frontend NOT tested per review request scope.
rca_of_issue: N/A — no bug. Verification-only run confirming deployment_agent fix (adding --tunnel flag) did not break backend.
